data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; t1_output.json (carry_for_later_tests)

Q30.r: No legally binding or scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment/regeneration rule with a defined non-compliance consequence is found for the commons resource (government nurseries, classified A2). The scheme's Official Notification (2024) specifies: (a) '60%+ survival target at 1-year' — this is a performance metric for privately planted trees, not a replenishment obligation for the commons (nursery) resource; (b) '3-year maintenance support' — advisory/extension support for private plantations, not a commons replenishment mandate; (c) nursery restocking is implicit in the annual budget cycle (₹20 cr nursery establishment per FY), but this is operational continuity, not a scheme-guideline-mandated replenishment rule with a stated consequence (fund withholding, disqualification, reduced allocation) for non-compliance. No scheme clause mandating nursery restocking targets with enforcement consequences was identified. RESULT: T4.1 = No → AUTOMATIC T4 FAIL (Near-SOC Structural). The scheme generates sapling distribution from public nursery resources but contains no structural lock against nursery depletion or afforestation shortfall.
Q30.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q31.r: Carried T1.1 = A2 (state-managed government nurseries — a physical/natural commons category). T1.1 ≠ E, therefore the ρ Ratio track applies. Q32 is answered; Q33 is set to SKIP.

Q32.calc: ρ = Annual extraction from commons ÷ Annual replenishment mandated/created by scheme. Proxy method (T1.5 = No; resource un-demarcated; Estimated): Extraction = saplings distributed from nursery stock per year = 2.5 crore (FY 2025-26 target, confirmed by CAG and Outcome Budget). Replenishment = new sapling production capacity per year = 2.5 crore (annual nursery production target per scheme design, ₹20 cr nursery establishment budget supporting 85 government nurseries producing 2.5 cr saplings). ρ = 2.5 cr ÷ 2.5 cr = 1.00.
Q32.interp: PASS on ratio — ρ = 1.00 ≤ 1.2 threshold. However, this result is Estimated (T1.5 = No; no demarcated resource boundary; proxy method used: annual distribution = extraction, annual production target = replenishment). NOTE: Although ρ ≤ 1.2 (ratio PASS), T4a overall FAIL is driven by Q30 (T4.1 = No — no mandatory replenishment rule with enforcement). The ρ calculation also reflects a design feature: the scheme produces saplings for distribution, so extraction and production are equal by design — this does not represent a genuine sustainability lock. Uncertainty: T1.5 = No means this ratio cannot be verified against measured boundary data; re-verify when nursery demarcation data and audited production vs distribution records are available.
Q32.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6
docs: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — annual distribution: 2.5 crore saplings confirmed
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — distribution records verified: 2.5 cr (FY 2024-25)
Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6 — nursery establishment budget ₹20 cr for 2.5 cr production

sources_verified: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department)
